subject: Public Health
title: Adverse Childhood Experiences and Addiction
description: A micro-lesson that makes Adverse Childhood Experiences and Addiction usable.

hook.text: A child who experiences abuse, neglect, or household dysfunction is 5 times more likely to develop addiction as.

buildup.text: The ACE study—tracking 17,000 adults—found a dose-response relationship: more childhood adversity, more addiction risk.

discovery.text: Trauma rewires the stress response system—substances become a coping mechanism for unbearable emotional pain.

twist.text: Treating addiction without addressing underlying trauma often fails—the pain that drives use remains untreated.

climax.text: Trauma-informed care—recognizing and addressing ACEs—improves addiction treatment outcomes significantly.

punchline.text: Addiction often isn't the problem. It's the solution to a deeper wound.

quiz.question: What did the ACE study find about childhood adversity and addiction?
options: More adversity means more addiction risk
